episodes.title: Signs of Coercive Control: How High Control Groups Exploit Our Need to Belong
episodes.description:
What happens when our universal human need for community and connection is hijacked by extreme groups? 

In this episode of The Chaplain’s Compass, Rev. John Betz and Rev. Alice Tremaine engage with Daniella Mestyanek Young @KnittingCultLady , a scholar of cults, military intelligence veteran, and author of "Uncultured" and "The Culting of America." Together, they explore the hidden mechanics of cultic behavior, from child exploitation masked as religious labor to the parallel control structures found in institutional life.

This conversation examines how professional chaplains address the subtle indicators of coercive groups, support individuals without becoming a replacement authority figure, and make space for people navigating deep spiritual struggles as they work to reclaim their autonomy, skills, and rituals.

In this episode:
00:00 Intro
08:07 Universal Signs of Coercive Control and Exploitation
15:04 Examining High Control Culture in Public Institutions
18:54 Redefining Cult Dynamics as Forced Labor Systems
25:29 Addressing Spiritual Distress and Decision Paralysis Symptoms
36:12 How Professional Chaplains Support Trauma Recovery Journeys
46:13 Religious Deconstruction Processes and Reclaiming Autonomy
59:03 Moving Past Religious Certainty and Rigid Structures
